title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Packet Lens: Net Route Address Faults
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net route address faults increment in an evidence-backed incident resolution packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose route table, default route, gateway, IP, duplicate address, subnet mask, and pool-exhaustion failures.
Diagnose loops, root selection, port roles and states, VLAN assignment, trunking, and ACL direction or order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Build Net Route Address Faults evidence
